title: Execution Model
use_for: choosing how stages execute once boundaries and products are defined
deciding between sequential and parallel stage execution
making runtime dispatch reflect the pipeline's data dependencies
rules: derive execution order from data dependencies
keep dispatch subordinate to stage architecture
parallelize only where dependencies and ownership allow it
prefer predictable execution over opaque opportunistic scheduling
procedure: Validate synchronization cost against throughput benefit.

primary_when: the request is specifically about how a data-oriented pipeline should execute, dispatch, or parallelize
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about stage ownership or source-versus-derived state

solves: how to turn a stage architecture into an execution plan
how to parallelize only the regions the data model allows

core_principles: Execution follows data dependency, not convenience.
Parallelism is a consequence of safe boundaries, not a substitute for them.
Dispatch should stay understandable during profiling and debugging.
constraints: Synchronization must be cheaper than the throughput it enables.
Execution order must remain explainable from the stage graph.
decision_rules: Run sequentially by default until a stage region is proven parallel-safe.
Use scheduler support only after stage ownership and invalidation boundaries are explicit.

preferred_patterns: parallel-safe regions derived from clean stage boundaries
explicit dispatch policy per stage group
avoid_patterns: scheduler-first architecture before stage design exists

recipes.name: Choose a dispatch model from stage dependencies
recipes.when: Use when stage boundaries are known but runtime execution is not.
steps: Find independent regions that share no unsafe mutable ownership.
Assign sequential or parallel dispatch by region.
Measure whether synchronization preserves real benefit.

pitfalls: Designing around the scheduler before stage boundaries exist.
Assuming parallelism without proving ownership separation.
Using dispatch complexity that obscures profiling.
review_checklist: Does execution order follow the stage graph?
Can profiling still explain where work ran and why?

references.title: Local Reference: Execution Model
references.interpretation: Use this first for dispatch choices derived from stage dependencies and ownership clarity.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for pipeline execution planning.
